title: Richly Truffle Saudi Arabian Vegetable Pilaf
description: A hearty, lazy-sunday Saudi Arabian dish featuring truffle oil and tandoor flavors, perfect for a relaxing meal. This ovo-vegetarian-friendly pilaf is made with fresh vegetables and aromatic spices.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of truffle oil in a large wok over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the grated carrots, sliced zucchini, and diced red bell pepper, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the tandoor spice blend and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the rinsed basmati rice to the wok, stirring to combine with the vegetables and spices.
Push the rice mixture to one side of the wok, and crack in the eggs, scrambling them until cooked through.
Mix the eggs with the rice mixture, and season with salt to taste.
Drizzle the remaining 1 tablespoon of truffle oil over the pilaf, and serve hot.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
